title: Revert "InstCombine: Fold is.fpclass(x, fcInf) to fabs+fcmp"
summary:
The generalization expanded a specific `is.fpclass(x, fcInf)` → `fcmp oeq fabs(x), +inf` optimization into a broader pattern that handles multiple predicates and class combinations. Originally, the transform only matched exact equality comparisons (`oeq`/`one`/`ueq`/`une`) against infinity. The patch removed this limitation, allowing other ordered/unordered comparisons (`oge`, `ole`, `olt`, `ord`) to also be folded into `is.fpclass` when combined with `ord` checks on `fabs(x)`. This recognizes that patterns like `(fcmp ord fabs(x), 0) & (fcmp oge fabs(x), inf)` are equivalent to `is.fpclass(x, fcInf)` because `fabs(x) ≥ inf` implies `fabs(x) == inf` for ordered values.

The key insight: any `fcmp` predicate that, when applied to `fabs(x)` relative to `inf`, guarantees equality under the `ord` condition can be canonicalized to `is.fpclass`. This unifies many test cases—handling `oge`, `ole`, `olt`, and `ord`—while preserving correctness by ensuring the `ord` check excludes NaNs. Developers can apply this by identifying when a combination of comparisons and logical ops collapses to a single FP class test, then generalizing across all predicates that imply the same final classification.
